schemes.name: Aditya Birla Capital Scholarship
schemes.eligibility: Students from Class 1 to PG; Min 60% marks in previous class; Family income ≤ ₹6 Lakh per annum.

schemes.name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship
schemes.description: Scholarship for girl students pursuing technical education in AICTE approved colleges.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be a female student.
Must be admitted to AICTE-approved technical institutions.
Course must be degree or diploma in technical education.
Family income should not exceed 8 lakh per year.
Maximum two girls per family can apply.
schemes.eligibility: Girl students in 1st year (or 2nd year lateral entry) of Degree/Diploma in AICTE colleges; Family income ≤ ₹8 Lakh per annum.

schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat Yojana
schemes.description: Free health insurance of ₹5 lakh per family for secondary and tertiary hospitalization.
schemes.eligibility: Families listed in SECC-2011 database; BPL (Below Poverty Line) card holders; specific occupational groups in urban areas.

schemes.name: Bank education loans under IBA norms
schemes.eligibility: Indian Nationals; Secured admission in India or abroad via entrance/merit; No age bar generally; Repayment capacity of parents.

schemes.name: Begum Hazrat Mahal National Scholarship
schemes.eligibility: Girl students from Minority communities (Muslim, Christian, Sikh, etc.); Class 9 to 12; Min 50% marks; Family income ≤ ₹2 Lakh.

schemes.name: Central Sector Interest Subsidy (CSIS)
features: Interest paid by government during study period + 1 year
cons: Usually limited to eligible technical/professional courses
schemes.eligibility: EWS students; Family income ≤ ₹4.5 Lakh; Admitted to recognized technical/professional courses in India; Loan from scheduled banks.

schemes.name: Central Sector Interest Subsidy Scheme (CSIS)
schemes.description: Provides interest subsidy on education loans where government pays interest during the moratorium period.
features: Applicable for professional and technical courses in India
Family income limit ₹4.5 lakh per year
Interest paid by government during study period (course duration + 1 year)
eligibility_criteria: Must belong to Economically Weaker Section (EWS)
Family income ≤ ₹4.5 lakh per year
Loan from scheduled bank in India
Enrolled in recognized professional/technical course

schemes.name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship for College and University Students
Central Sector Scholarship Scheme (PM-USP)
schemes.description: Financial assistance to meritorious students from low-income families.
features: Family income limit ₹4.5 lakh per year
eligibility_criteria: Top 20% of Class 12 board results
Family income ≤ ₹4.5 lakh per year
Regular UG/PG course in recognized institution
Maintain good academic performance

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme for Education Loans (CGFSEL)
schemes.description: Allows banks to provide loans without collateral because the government provides guarantee support.
eligibility_criteria: Apply through participating bank
Loan amount generally up to ₹7.5 lakh
Admitted to recognized higher education course
Follows IBA education loan guidelines

schemes.name: Digital India Programme
schemes.description: Umbrella programme for a digitally empowered society.
schemes.eligibility: All Indian Citizens; Focus on digital literacy and e-governance access.

schemes.name: Dr. Ambedkar Central Sector Scheme of Interest Subsidy
features: Interest subsidy on overseas education loans
schemes.eligibility: OBC and EBC students; Pursuing Overseas Masters/M.Phil/PhD; Family income ≤ ₹5 Lakh (OBC) or ≤ ₹2.5 Lakh (EBC).

schemes.name: HDFC Bank Parivartan ECSS
schemes.eligibility: Students from Class 1 to PG; Facing crisis (death of parent/medical emergency/natural disaster); Family income ≤ ₹2.5 Lakh; Min 55% marks.

schemes.name: HDFC Educational Crisis Scholarship
schemes.description: For students facing financial crisis due to loss of parent, medical emergency, or natural disaster.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be an Indian student studying in school or college.
Must face sudden financial crisis such as: Loss of parent/guardian, Serious illness, or Natural disaster.
Must show proof of financial hardship.

schemes.name: Indira Gandhi Single Girl Child Scholarship
schemes.eligibility: Only girl child in the family; Admitted to 1st year full-time PG course in a recognized university; Age limit up to 30 years.

schemes.name: INSPIRE Scholarship (SHE)
schemes.description: Scholarship for Higher Education in natural and basic sciences.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be among the top 1% performers in Class 12 board examinations.
Must pursue BSc, BS, or Integrated MSc/MS in natural sciences.
Course must be taken in a recognized university or institute in India.
Age generally between 17-22 years.
schemes.eligibility: Top 1% in Class 12 Board; Pursuing Natural/Basic Sciences (B.Sc/M.Sc); Age 17-22 years; or JEE/NEET/KVPY rankers.

schemes.name: Ishan Uday Scholarship
schemes.eligibility: Domicile of North Eastern States (NER); Pursuing first degree course; Family income ≤ ₹4.5 Lakh; Passing Class 12 equivalent.

schemes.name: Kasturba Gandhi Balika Vidyalaya (KGBV)
pros: Very strong support for rural girls
schemes.eligibility: Girls from SC, ST, OBC, Minority communities, or BPL families; Aged 10-18; Focus on out-of-school/dropout girls in rural blocks.

schemes.name: Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana (KVPY)
schemes.description: Fellowship for research careers in Science and Tech.
eligibility_criteria: Student must have strong interest in science research.
Must be studying in Class 11, 12, or undergraduate science courses.
Must qualify the KVPY aptitude test and interview.
Must maintain required minimum marks in science subjects.
schemes.eligibility: Class 11, 12 or 1st year UG (Science); Must clear KVPY Aptitude Test and Interview; Min 60-75% in Science/Maths based on stream.

schemes.name: Kotak Kanya Scholarship
schemes.eligibility: Girl students admitted to first-year UG (Professional courses like Engg, Medical, Law); Family income ≤ ₹6 Lakh; Min 85% in Class 12.

schemes.name: L'Oréal India For Young Women in Science
schemes.eligibility: Women passed Class 12 Science in current year; Min 85% in PCB/PCM; Family income ≤ ₹6 Lakh; Pursuing UG in Science/Medical/Engg.

schemes.name: Merit-cum-Means Scholarship for Professional and Technical Courses
pros: Useful for engineering and similar programs
cons: Needs strong marks and eligibility compliance

schemes.name: National Means-cum-Merit Scholarship (NMMS)
features: Secondary education support to prevent dropouts
schemes.eligibility: Class 9 students; Qualified through NMMS exam; Family income ≤ ₹3.5 Lakh; Studying in Govt/Aided schools.

schemes.name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP)
schemes.description: Single point application for multiple Govt schemes.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be an Indian citizen.
Must be studying in recognized schools or colleges in India.
Family income limit depends on the specific scholarship scheme.
Student must satisfy academic performance requirements.
Aadhaar number and bank account are usually required.
schemes.eligibility: Varies by scheme (Pre-matric, Post-matric, MCM); Generally requires being a regular student and meeting specific income/category norms.

schemes.name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP) schemes
features: Single portal for central and state scholarships

schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
schemes.description: ₹6,000/year direct income support.
schemes.eligibility: Small and Marginal Farmer families with cultivable landholding; Excludes institutional landholders and taxpayers.

schemes.name: PM Vidyalaxmi / PM-Vidyalakshmi
features: Compare and apply to multiple banks
cons: Approval still depends on bank rules

schemes.name: PM-Vidyalaxmi Scheme (Loan)
schemes.description: Provides collateral-free and guarantor-free education loans to students pursuing higher education in India.
eligibility_criteria: Indian citizen
Admission in recognized higher education institution in India based on merit/entrance
Application through Vidya Lakshmi Portal
Family income up to ₹8 lakh for subsidy
schemes.eligibility: Admitted to top 860+ Quality Higher Education Institutions (QHEI); Interest subsidy for family income ≤ ₹8 Lakh; Indian citizen.

schemes.name: PM-YASASVI / OBC-EBC-DNT scholarships
features: Support for OBC, EBC, and DNT students

schemes.name: Post-Matric Minority Scholarship
features: For minority students in higher classes and college

schemes.name: Post-Matric SC/ST Scholarship
schemes.description: Covers tuition fees, maintenance allowance, and other expenses for SC/ST category students.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must belong to SC or ST category.
Must be studying in post-matric courses (Class 11 and above).
Family income should generally be below 2.5 lakh per year (may vary slightly by state).
Student must be enrolled in a recognized institution.

schemes.name: Post-Matric Scholarship for Students with Disabilities
features: Maintenance and tuition fees for PwD
schemes.eligibility: Benchmark disability (40%+); Family income ≤ ₹2.5 Lakh; Pursuing Class 11 to PG; Disability certificate from medical authority required.

schemes.name: Pragati Scholarship Scheme
pros: Excellent for girls in technical fields
schemes.eligibility: Girl students in AICTE colleges; Degree/Diploma 1st year; Family income ≤ ₹8 Lakh; Max 2 girls per family.

schemes.name: Prime Minister’s Scholarship Schemes
features: For wards of CAPF, police martyrs, APS, and similar groups

schemes.name: Reliance Foundation Postgraduate Scholarship
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be an Indian citizen.
Must be enrolled in undergraduate degree programs.
Must demonstrate merit and leadership potential.
Family income limit generally around 15 lakh per year.

schemes.name: Reliance Foundation Scholarship
schemes.description: Merit-cum-means scholarship for undergraduate students across India in top institutions.

schemes.name: Reliance Foundation Undergraduate Scholarship
features: Up to ₹2 Lakh over the degree
schemes.eligibility: 1st year UG students in any stream; Min 60% in Class 12; Family income ≤ ₹15 Lakh; Resident Indian.

schemes.name: Santoor Women's Scholarship
schemes.eligibility: Women from disadvantaged backgrounds; Passed Class 10/12 from Govt school; Pursuing 3-year UG course; Karnataka/AP/Telangana/Chhattisgarh.

schemes.name: Sitaram Jindal Foundation
schemes.description: Monthly scholarships for students from economically disadvantaged backgrounds across all levels.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be an Indian student.
Must belong to economically weaker background.
Must have minimum required academic marks depending on education level.
Scholarship available for school, college, and professional courses.

schemes.name: Skill India Mission / PMKVY
schemes.eligibility: Indian youth (15-45 years); Unemployed or school/college dropouts; Aadhaar-seeded bank account; Specific criteria per course.

schemes.name: Startup India Scheme
schemes.description: Tax exemptions and funding support for startups.
schemes.eligibility: DPIIT Recognized Startup; Entity age < 10 years; Turnover < ₹100 Crore; Innovation/improvement focus.

schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
schemes.description: Savings scheme for girl child with tax benefits.
schemes.eligibility: Girl child aged 0-10 years; Account opened by parents/legal guardian; Resident Indian.

schemes.name: Swami Vivekananda Scholarship (SVMCM)
schemes.description: Merit-cum-means scholarship for WB students.
eligibility_criteria: Student must be a resident of West Bengal.
Must have minimum required marks in previous examination (usually around 60%).
Family income should be below 2.5 lakh per year.
Must be studying in recognized institutions.
schemes.eligibility: Domicile of West Bengal; Min 60% marks in previous exam; Family income ≤ ₹2.5 Lakh; Pursuing UG/PG/Professional courses.

schemes.name: Swanath Scholarship Scheme
schemes.eligibility: Orphans / Children of parents who died of COVID-19 / Wards of Armed Forces/Paramilitary martyred; Admitted to AICTE Degree/Diploma.

schemes.name: Tata Trusts Scholarships
schemes.description: Need and merit-based tuition support.
eligibility_criteria: Applicant must be an Indian student.
Must show financial need.
Must have good academic performance.
Admission must be in recognized institutions in India or abroad.
schemes.eligibility: Indian students pursuing Bachelor's, Master's or Specializations (Medical, Nursing, etc.); Merit-based; Specific cut-offs apply per stream.

schemes.name: PM Vidyalaxmi / Vidya Lakshmi Portal
schemes.description: Portal to apply for loans to multiple banks via one form.
eligibility_criteria: Register on portal
Fill Common Education Loan Application Form (CELAF)
Confirmed admission in recognized institution
Submission of ID proof, academic records, and admission proof
schemes.eligibility: Indian Citizen; Secured admission in Higher Education; Register on portal and fill Common Education Loan Application Form (CELAF).

schemes.name: Central Sector Scheme of Scholarship (PM-USP)
schemes.eligibility: Top 20th percentile of successful candidates in relevant Class 12 board; Family income ≤ ₹4.5 Lakh; Pursuing regular UG/PG course.

schemes.name: Credit Guarantee Fund Scheme (CGFSEL)
schemes.eligibility: Education loans up to ₹7.5 Lakh; No collateral or third-party guarantee required; Admitted to recognized higher education courses.
schemes.description: Government provides guarantee support to banks to allow collateral-free loans.

schemes.name: Merit-cum-Means Scholarship (Minorities)
schemes.eligibility: Minority communities; Pursuing Professional/Technical courses; Min 50% in previous exam; Family income ≤ ₹2.5 Lakh.

schemes.name: PM-YASASVI Scholarship
schemes.eligibility: OBC, EBC, and DNT students; Class 9 or 11; Family income ≤ ₹2.5 Lakh; Must pass the YASASVI Entrance Test.

Govt_Schemes.note: Converted from the detailed scheme lists above. Since the source lists were broad and some fields were approximate, I kept values as structured as possible without inventing extra precision.

government_schemes.name: PM Kisan Samman Nidhi
government_schemes.description: ₹6,000/year financial support to farmer families across India in three equal installments of ₹2,000 each.

government_schemes.name: Ayushman Bharat Yojana
government_schemes.description: Free health insurance of ₹5 lakh per family for secondary and tertiary hospitalization at empanelled hospitals.

government_schemes.name: Sukanya Samriddhi Yojana
government_schemes.description: Savings scheme for girl child with high interest rate of 8.2% and tax benefits under Section 80C.

government_schemes.name: Startup India Scheme
government_schemes.description: Tax exemptions, funding support, and simplified compliance for DPIIT-recognized startups in India.

government_schemes.name: Digital India Programme
government_schemes.description: Umbrella programme to transform India into a digitally empowered society with e-governance and digital literacy.

government.name: National Scholarship Portal (NSP)
government.description: Central portal for all government scholarship schemes including pre-matric, post-matric and merit-cum-means.

government.name: INSPIRE Scholarship

government.name: Post-Matric SC/ST Scholarship

government.name: AICTE Pragati Scholarship

government.name: Kishore Vaigyanik Protsahan Yojana
government.description: Fellowship to encourage students to take up research careers in science and technology.

government.name: Swami Vivekananda Scholarship

private.name: Tata Trusts Scholarships

private.name: Reliance Foundation Scholarship

private.name: Sitaram Jindal Foundation

private.name: HDFC Educational Crisis Scholarship
private.description: For students facing financial crisis due to loss of parent, medical emergency, or natural disaster.
